title: How to Report a Phishing or Suspicious Email
text:
Q: What should I do if I receive a phishing or suspicious email?
A: If you receive a phishing or suspicious email, it's important to report it promptly to help protect yourself and your colleagues. Windows: Step 1: Do not click on any links or download attachments in the email. Step 2: Open your email client (e.g., Outlook) and locate the suspicious email. Step 3: Click on the 'Report' button, usually found in the toolbar, and select 'Phishing' or 'Junk.' Step 4: If prompted, provide any additional details about why you believe the email is suspicious. macOS: Step 1: Avoid clicking on any links or downloading attachments. Step 2: Open your email application (e.g., Apple Mail) and find the suspicious email. Step 3: Click on the 'Message' menu at the top, then select 'Mark as Junk' or 'Report Phishing.' Step 4: Follow any prompts to provide further information if required. Additionally, you can forward the email to the IT helpdesk at helpdesk@company.com for further investigation. Always remember to check the sender's email address and look for signs of phishing, such as poor grammar or urgent requests for personal information.
